meta_title: For Students: debugging with AI to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Debugging with AI to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no portfolio' barrier by mastering AI-assisted debugging. A beginner's guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your first project.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap is solved by shipping real-world projects, not tutorials. By leveraging AI-driven debugging with Antigravity and Claude Code, you can resolve complex errors instantly, maintain velocity, and transform incomplete code snippets into polished, deployable portfolio pieces that showcase your technical problem-solving skills to recruiters.
content.intro:
The screen glows at 2 AM. You have the vision for a SaaS app, but your terminal is a graveyard of stack trace errors. You’re stuck in the 'tutorial loop,' watching others build while your own portfolio remains empty. The frustration isn't that you lack talent; it's that you lack the senior-level debugging intuition that usually takes years to acquire.

But the rules of the game have changed. By integrating AI into your development workflow, you aren't just writing code—you are orchestrating it. This case study shows you how to bypass the wall of technical debt and ship your first project, turning debugging from a roadblock into your greatest competitive advantage.

steps.title: Define Your MVP Scope
steps.description: Stop building 'everything' and focus on a single core feature. Clear constraints prevent scope creep and make debugging manageable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: I am a student building a [Feature Name] for my portfolio. Act as a Senior Architect and help me define a minimal, high-impact scope that proves my technical competency to recruiters.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --init --project-scope 'Build a task manager MVP with local storage and CRUD operations.'

steps.title: Scaffold with AI Assistance
steps.description: Use AI to generate the boilerplate code so you can focus on logic rather than syntax setup.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust, clean boilerplate for a [Tech Stack] application. Ensure modular file structure and include comments explaining the directory architecture.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a React/Node boilerplate with a clean folder structure and basic routing.'

steps.title: Debug Logic with Claude Code
steps.description: When an error hits, don't guess. Use Claude Code to analyze the stack trace and suggest a fix based on your project context.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this error: [Paste Error]. Explain the root cause in simple terms and provide the corrected code snippet.
steps.claude_code_command: claude debug 'Explain why the state is not updating in the UserComponent and propose a fix.'

steps.title: Refactor for Readability
steps.description: Code is read more often than written. Use AI to clean your code so it looks professional to potential employers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this code to follow clean code principles. Add JSDoc comments and optimize for performance.
steps.claude_code_command: claude refactor 'Apply clean code principles to the auth middleware and add documentation.'

steps.title: Document for Deployment
steps.description: A README is the difference between a project and a portfolio piece. Use AI to document your journey.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a professional README.md for my project. Include a 'Problem Solved' section, 'Tech Stack' list, and 'How to Run' instructions.

pro_tips: Treat AI as a junior partner, not a god: Always verify logic with a simple unit test before committing.
Master the Stack Trace: Use Claude to explain the 'why' behind an error, not just the fix, to build your own mental model.
Context is King: When debugging, always provide the related component files to the AI to prevent hallucinated fixes.
Build for the 'Recruiter Read': Use AI to ensure your variable names and folder structures follow industry-standard conventions.
Version Control Everything: Create a git branch before letting an AI refactor your code so you can always revert.
content.next_steps: Once your project is deployed, record a 60-second Loom video walking through the code, embed it in your README, and share it on LinkedIn tagging your tech stack community.
content.conversion_block: You now have the tools, the workflow, and the mindset to stop dreaming and start shipping. Every line of code you write today is one step closer to your first developer role. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
